caption: FIG. 1. Modeled (Ref. 36) coverage pro /uniFB01 les for a varied precursor dose (a) and initial sticking probability (b). Experimentally, such coverage pro /uniFB01 les are acquired as normalized thickness pro /uniFB01 les of /uniFB01 lms deposited in LHAR structures (Refs. 7, 14, 28, and 29) of which a schematic cross-sectional side view is given in panel (c). Panel (d) gives the extracted relation between the slope at the pro /uniFB01 le front and the value of the initial sticking probability.
image_path: /home/ftk3187/github/PSED/0604_kg/output/Arts et al. - 2019 - Sticking probabilities of H2O and Al(CH3)3 during atomic layer deposition of Al2O3 extracted from th/01_docling/figures/figure-008.png

caption: FIG. 2. Normalized thickness pro /uniFB01 les of Al2O3 deposited in LHAR3 structures with varied TMA dosing, as obtained by Ylilammi et al . (left, 300 °C) (Ref. 14) and in this work ( Tset = 275 °C). When the penetration depth of TMA is lower than that of H2O (called TMA-limited, middle), the pro /uniFB01 le has a sharper front than when TMA penetrates deeper than H2O (called H2O-limited, right). This can be attributed to the higher sticking probability of TMA compared to H2O.
image_path: /home/ftk3187/github/PSED/0604_kg/output/Arts et al. - 2019 - Sticking probabilities of H2O and Al(CH3)3 during atomic layer deposition of Al2O3 extracted from th/01_docling/figures/figure-009.png

caption: FIG. 3. Panel (a) shows thickness pro /uniFB01 les of Al2O3 deposited at different set temperatures, where the penetration depth of TMA is higher than that of H2O. For these H2O-limited pro /uniFB01 les, the temperature-dependent reactivity of H2O is re /uniFB02 ected in the increasing slope at the pro /uniFB01 le front. The initial sticking probabilities of H2O corresponding to these slopes are plotted against the substrate temperature in panel (b). Due to limited thermal contact, the substrate temperatures of ∼ 150, ∼ 220, and ∼ 310 °C are typically lower than the set table temperatures of 150, 275, and 400 °C and are therefore estimated based on calibration. The determined values of s 0 (dotted line) show a similar trend as those obtained by Vandalon et al . using SFG (solid line) (Ref. 15).
